_meta.description: Ocean-related observances, BC (British Columbia, Canada) holidays, and BC/transboundary Pacific salmon & herring run info for Strong Coast's Slack reminder bot.

date_type_reference.fixed: params: {month, day} - same calendar date every year
date_type_reference.nth_weekday: params: {month, weekday(0=Mon..6=Sun), n} - nth occurrence of weekday in month
date_type_reference.last_weekday_of_month: params: {month, weekday} - last occurrence of weekday in month
date_type_reference.before_fixed_date: params: {weekday, before_month, before_day} - the Monday (or given weekday) on/preceding a fixed date, going backwards (used for Victoria Day: Monday preceding May 25)
date_type_reference.before_easter: params: {offset_days} - offset_days relative to Easter Sunday (negative = before)

optional_fields.year_parity: 'odd' or 'even' - event only fires in years of that parity (e.g. Fraser/Campbell pink salmon runs are dominant in odd years; World Fish Migration Day is biennial on even years)
optional_fields.recurrence_filter: {type: 'cycle', start_year, interval} - event only fires in years where (year - start_year) % interval == 0 (e.g. Adams River dominant sockeye run, every 4th year from 2026)
optional_fields.notice_days_override: integer - use this heads-up window instead of the script's default --notice-days for this specific event

_meta.excluded_by_design: Liard River and Finlay River were requested but are NOT included: both drain to the Arctic Ocean via the Mackenzie/Peace watershed (Finlay's historic outlet is now blocked by the W.A.C. Bennett Dam/Williston Lake) and have no natural anadromous Pacific salmon runs, so there is no recurring salmon-run date to track for them.

events.name: Nass River Oolichan (Eulachon) Run
events.note: The Nass River's late-winter oolichan ('salvation fish') run is one of the largest and most culturally significant on the BC coast for the Nisga'a Nation; timing is ice/temperature dependent, roughly Feb-March.

events.name: Pacific Herring Spawn (Strait of Georgia)
events.note: Herring spawn along Vancouver Island/Strait of Georgia typically runs mid-Feb through April (Denman/Hornby Islands, Baynes Sound, Neck Point are prime viewing); exact days are natural-event dependent, check DFO spawn bulletins closer to the date.

events.name: World Whale Day
events.note: Pacific Whale Foundation's week-long Maui festival culminating in the World Whale Day Parade; widely marked as an international whale-awareness day. Confirm exact dates at pacificwhale.org each year.

events.name: World Wetlands Day
events.note: Marks the 1971 Ramsar Convention; relevant to BC's coastal estuaries and salmon-bearing wetlands.

events.name: World Wildlife Day
events.note: UN day for wild flora/fauna, includes marine species like BC's orcas and salmon.

events.name: International Day for Biological Diversity
events.note: UN biodiversity day; ties to BC's coastal and marine ecosystems.

events.name: World Migratory Bird Day (Spring)
events.note: 2nd Saturday of May; BC's coast (Fraser Estuary, Boundary Bay) is a major Pacific Flyway stopover for shorebirds and seabirds.

events.name: World Fish Migration Day
events.note: Global, biennial (even years) day highlighting free-flowing rivers and migratory fish like BC's Pacific salmon; confirm exact date at worldfishmigrationday.com as it is chosen per cycle, not a fixed weekday rule.

events.name: Victoria Day
events.note: BC statutory holiday; unofficial start of BC's boating/fishing season on the coast.

events.name: Campbell River Chinook Season begins
events.note: Campbell River, the 'Salmon Capital of the World' (Vancouver Island), sees Chinook May-August.

events.name: Stikine & Taku Transboundary Chinook Run
events.note: BC-Alaska transboundary rivers; Chinook run May-July, spawning mainly in the Tahltan/Iskut/Chutine (Stikine) tributaries. The Taku is the single largest Chinook/sockeye producer of the two.

events.name: Skeena River Salmon Run begins
events.note: BC's second-largest salmon river; DFO's Tyee Test Fishery standardizes its season start to June 10 each year. Chinook run May-July, sockeye and pink peak July-August, coho August-September.

events.name: Chinook Salmon Run begins (BC rivers, general)
events.note: Chinook (king) salmon run BC rivers June-October depending on river; Harrison River run is especially notable.

events.name: World Reef Awareness Day
events.note: Global reef conservation awareness day.

events.name: Orca Awareness Month begins
events.note: June is Orca Awareness Month across the Pacific Northwest, highlighting BC's Southern and Northern Resident killer whales.

events.name: Salmon Calling Ceremony (Columbia River)
events.note: Okanagan Nation Alliance / Columbia River Basin Nations' annual ceremony marking Indigenous-led salmon reintroduction work; Columbia salmon have had no natural access to the Canadian portion of the river since Grand Coulee Dam was built in 1942 with no fish passage. Exact dates vary each June — confirm at syilx.org.

events.name: World Environment Day
events.note: UN environment day, frequently ocean-themed in recent years.

events.name: World Oceans Day
events.note: The flagship UN ocean observance, celebrated with events at BC aquariums and coastal communities.

events.name: World Sea Turtle Day
events.note: Global sea turtle conservation day.

events.name: International Albatross Day
events.note: Seabird conservation day relevant to BC's offshore albatross populations.

events.name: National Indigenous Peoples Day
events.note: Federal observance; deeply tied to BC coastal First Nations' relationship with salmon, herring, and the sea.

events.name: Canada Day
events.note: Federal statutory holiday, widely celebrated on BC's beaches and waterfronts.

events.name: Nass River Sockeye Run begins
events.note: The Nass is BC's third-largest sockeye river (after the Fraser and Skeena), managed jointly with the Nisga'a Nation; sockeye arrive early summer.

events.name: Stikine & Taku Transboundary Sockeye/Coho Run
events.note: Sockeye peak mid-summer (largest Stikine spawning grounds at Tahltan Lake); pink and chum spawn in August; coho follow September-October.

events.name: Shark Awareness Day
events.note: Global shark conservation awareness day.

events.name: Sockeye Salmon Run begins (Fraser River & interior lakes)
events.note: Fraser sockeye return Aug-Oct, peaking in October; the Fraser Panel (psc.org) posts in-season run updates. Runs are dramatically larger in dominant years (2026, 2030...).

events.name: BC Day
events.note: BC-only statutory holiday (1st Monday of August); major coastal/beach long weekend.

events.name: Pink Salmon Run (dominant odd-year cycle)
events.note: Pink (humpback) salmon run Fraser River, Campbell River, and south-coast rivers Aug-Sept in odd years (2025, 2027...), with hundreds of thousands of fish at peak.

events.name: Campbell River Coho Season begins
events.note: Coho run Campbell River August-October; chum also present in fall in smaller numbers.

events.name: International Whale Shark Day
events.note: Global whale shark conservation day.

events.name: Labour Day
events.note: Federal/BC statutory holiday marking the end of summer boating season.

events.name: International Coastal Cleanup Day
events.note: 3rd Saturday of September; anchor day for Ocean Wise's Great Canadian Shoreline Cleanup, heavily run along BC's coast.

events.name: National Day for Truth and Reconciliation
events.note: Federal/BC statutory holiday; strong ties to coastal First Nations communities.

events.name: BC Rivers Day (World Rivers Day)
events.note: Last Sunday of September; province-wide celebration of BC's rivers and watersheds feeding the Pacific.

events.name: World Migratory Bird Day (Fall)
events.note: 2nd Saturday of October; fall counterpart covering southbound shorebird/seabird migration through BC's coast.

events.name: Coho Salmon Run begins (BC coastal rivers, general)
events.note: Coho (silver) salmon run BC coastal streams October-November; popular on Vancouver Island and Lower Mainland rivers.

events.name: Salute to the Sockeye Festival (Adams River)
events.note: Dominant-year sockeye run at Roderick Haig-Brown Provincial Park (Adams River), only every 4th year (2026, 2030, 2034...); confirm exact festival dates at salmonsociety.com (2026: Oct 9-25).

events.name: Chum Salmon Run peaks (Goldstream & coastal rivers)
events.note: Chum (dog) salmon run peaks mid-October through early December at Goldstream Provincial Park and coastal streams; eagles concentrate here in large numbers.

events.name: Thanksgiving Day
events.note: Federal/BC statutory holiday; coincides with BC's fall salmon runs.

events.name: World Fisheries Day
events.note: Global day recognizing sustainable fisheries, directly relevant to BC's commercial and Indigenous fisheries.

events.name: Remembrance Day
events.note: Federal/BC statutory holiday.

events.name: New Year's Day
events.note: Federal/BC statutory holiday.

events.name: Family Day
events.note: BC statutory holiday (3rd Monday of February).

events.name: Good Friday
events.note: Federal/BC statutory holiday.

events.name: Christmas Day
events.note: Federal/BC statutory holiday.
